posts.author: Tapology
posts.body: The official Tapology discussion thread for the event!

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.body: Predictions: 10 of 10 Pending | Tied for 1st with 82 others Azeredo vs. OrazgeldyevBakhtiyar Orazgeldyev, TKO, R2I have no clue who this guy is but he seems like he has a pulse so this could get scary. Azeredo is old and not very good but he gets preferential treatment in UAEW because he knows someone in the promotion. He’s given main/co main in every fight and never faces steps up in comp. I assume this guy either looks like a can on tape or is taking a dive (probably both) but I will never pick Azevedo against someone with a pulseAmirzhanov vs. RochaUllubiy Amirzhanov, SUB, R1Glad to see Ullubiy back. I have a very bad feeling he will sign with the PFL next but this seems like a dwcs eliminatior. Seems like a good test as well, Rocha doesn’t look like a compete jobber. All his wins are by ko and considering the gym he train at, I assume he has a striking background (everyone at TLM competed in KB/MT/boxing). Ullubiy is such a unique prospect to me. There’s still some questions/concerns I have but he also has an insane amount of tools at his disposal. For starters, he’s 6”0 at BW; just crazy. He has a lot of subs but he’s actually a very competent striker. His dad is a credentialed kickboxing coach back in Dagestan, so despite the bjj he’s been training striking his whole life. I do worry about physicality since he’s so frail, but so far it hasn’t been an issue. I also worry about his wrestling a little, as he’s far too content to get take. Down; he knows how to grapple but it could be a real problem if he makes it to the ufc. I also worry about cardio as well since he cuts a ton of weight and finishes everyone but at the same time, he fights reserved so I assume he’ll be fine. Wrestling shouldn’t be an issue against a striker, so I expect Ullubiy to find takedowns. His bjj is crazy dynamic; I usually consider Buggy chokes as a massive red flag but the way he set it up was crazy impressive. Again, I don’t think this flys at the ufc level but he locked up the buggy and then immediately moved to a mounted Buggy; that’s how people should be attempting them and it speaks to his overall level. I expect an eas finis here; please do the right thing and bring him on dwcs; this will be a horrendous fumble by ufc if they let another club abdulmanap guy walkSaid vs. Henrique da SilvaMuhammad Said, TKO, R1Nice the ex ufc test now for Said. He did not impress me at all last fight but he’s training at Gor MMA(ufc loves these guys) an repping UAE so I assume he is pushing for a dwcs shot. He looked very mid against da Silva so I don’t have high hope here. Da Silva is a punching bag with bad cardio though, so I don’t see Said having many issuesBessala vs. DumisoAyissi Bessala, TKO, R1Loving the activity from Bessala. His last fight was not pretty but that’s to be expected when you’re only 2-0 with no prior experience. The guy he fought was bad but at least he had multiple wins. Bessala is still figuring things out but he is a freak athlete and very explosive. He reminds me of Ateba, not just because they’re both Cameroonian lol. He’s also managed by Ali so I imagine he’s going to the big leagues once he gets to 5/6-0Mohey vs. AliRizwan Ali, DECAli is a mega fraud but uaew is giving him a layup. Mohey’s record is somehow just as padded, and he trains in Egypt; one of the most fraudulent regional scenes out there
